title: Why React Remains My Top Choice in 2025
author: Mohammad Saleh
excerpt: React continues to dominate frontend development. Here’s why I choose it for most of my projects...
content:
React is still the most productive frontend library I've used. With the introduction of Server Components and improved performance, it's a no-brainer for modern web apps.

In 2025, the ecosystem is richer than ever: Next.js, Remix, and Tailwind work seamlessly. The component model keeps my code organized, and the developer experience is unmatched.

If you're starting web development, learn React. It opens doors to freelancing, full-time jobs, and your own projects.

title: 5 Tailwind CSS Tips for Faster Development
author: Mohammad Saleh
excerpt: Tailwind CSS has changed how I style websites. These tips will help you build UIs faster and keep code clean...
content:
Tailwind CSS utility classes are powerful, but here are a few tricks I've learned:

  • Use @apply sparingly – only for repeating complex patterns.
  • Leverage arbitrary values for one-off designs.
  • Configure your theme wisely – add custom colors, fonts, and screens.
  • Use `group` and `peer` for complex interactions.
  • Combine with `clsx` or `class-variance-authority` for dynamic classes.

With these, you'll ship responsive, consistent UIs in half the time.

title: From Madrasha Student to Self‑Taught Developer
author: Mohammad Saleh
excerpt: My journey from studying in a religious institution to becoming a web developer. Resources and mindset that helped me...
content:
It wasn't an easy path. I studied Dakhil (secondary) and Alim (higher secondary) from a madrasha. While I learned discipline and values, there was no computer science. So I taught myself.

Here's what worked:

  1. freeCodeCamp – foundation of HTML/CSS/JS.
  2. Building projects – I built 10+ small websites before taking clients.
  3. WordPress first – easy way to earn while learning React.
  4. Joining developer communities – Twitter, Discord, LinkedIn.
  5. Consistency – 2 hours daily, no excuses.

Today, I earn as a freelance developer and mentor others. Your background doesn't limit you – your effort does.
